channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope remains stable after month in hospital as he continues treatments and works from bedside
item.description: Pope Francis continues to improve a month after he was hospitalized with bronchitis and double pneumonia, the Vatican’s Holy See Press Office said in an update on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's pick for hostage negotiations envoy withdraws nomination but will continue on hostage issues
item.description: Adam Boehler, President Donald Trump’s nominee to become special envoy for hostage affairs, has withdrawn his nomination to avoid a requirement that he divest from his investment business.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba suffered a massive power outage Friday night affecting Havana and the country's provinces that left millions in the dark. Cuba’s Ministry of Energy and Mines said at around 8:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has been killed, Iraqi prime minister says
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — The head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has been killed in Iraq in an operation by members of the Iraqi national intelligence service along with U.S.-led coalition forces, the Iraqi prime minister announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio declares South African ambassador to US 'persona non grata' over Trump comments
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio declared South African ambassador to the U.S. Ebrahim Rasool “persona non grata” and accused the diplomat of being a “race-baiting politician” over his recent comments about President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Greenland political parties join together against Trump comments about annexing the island
item.description: LONDON (AP) — All five parties in Greenland’s parliament issued a joint statement on Friday rejecting President Donald Trump’s latest effort to take control of the strategic Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Most Americans disapprove of President Trump’s approach to the country's relationship with Russia and his handling of the situation in Ukraine, according to a new survey published on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Hamas says it will release American hostage, agrees to restart Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: Hamas said Friday that it agreed to release American Israeli hostage Edan Alexander and the bodies of four other Americans, following a proposal from mediators to resume negotiations with Israel over a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Dozens of Syrian Druze make a rare visit to Israeli-controlled Golan Heights
item.description: MAJDAL SHAMS, Golan Heights (AP) — Dozens of clerics and others from Syria’s minority Druze community crossed into the Israeli-controlled side of the Golan Heights Friday for the first time in decades.
item.dc:creator: BY ALON BERNSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover possible mass killing site in Jalisco state
item.description: TEUCHITLAN, Mexico (AP) — When a group of citizens searching for missing relatives in the western state of Jalisco arrived at a remote ranch outside Mexico's second-largest city last week on an anonymous tip, all they had to do was push open the unlocked gate.
item.dc:creator: DIANA DE LA MORA and FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin will always be a threat to stability in Europe: Dissident
item.description: Ukrainian leaders have already agreed to a 30-day halt in fighting, and now, U.S. diplomats are seeking Russia’s acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: UN official denies seeing 'a shred of evidence' showing staff in Gaza held hostages
item.description: Tom Fletcher, a top UN humanitarian aid official, says he has not seen "a shred of evidence" linking the international body's workers in Gaza to Hamas hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin thanks Trump for principles of ceasefire push, but does not say yes
item.description: Russian President Putin says he agrees in "principle" with President Trump's desire for a 30-day ceasefire but argued it would only help Ukraine and said he wants a lasting peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu slams 'terrorist-supporting' UN council that accused Israel of committing sexual crimes
item.description: Israeli and American officials are condemning a report from the United Nations Human Rights Council that accuses Israel of carrying out systemic acts of sexual violence against Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis is stable and showing improvements but will remain hospitalized
item.description: Pope Francis' health has taken a turn for the better this week as he continues to show improvement following a Monday update that listed him as “out of danger from death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 passengers in restive southwestern Pakistan killed 21 hostages before security forces killed all 33 of the assailants, and all other passengers were rescued, officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia hits Zelenskyy's hometown as Ukraine signals it's ready for peace
item.description: As Ukraine appeared to pivot towards peace, Russia launched a deadly attack on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown of Kryvvi Rih.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope gets good news from doctors: He's no longer in immediate danger
item.description: Doctors are still cautious and have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman allegedly locked naked partner on balcony, leading to his death
item.description: Police say the woman locked her partner outside overnight after a fight amid temperatures of 38 degrees Fahrenheit.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope follows Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as recovery continues
item.description: Pasolini delivered a meditation on “The hope of eternal life,” a theme that was chosen well before Francis was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital on Feb. 14 with a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
